demos.title: Phase Delay Reference
demos.description: Two delayed minimum-phase FIR channels for validating group delay, phase delay, and aligned impulse reconstruction.
demos.sourceSummary: Perfect white circular excitation mirrored to both outputs.
demos.systemSummary: Two channels with distinct delayed minimum-phase FIR responses.
demos.notes: Channel 0 delay is 48 samples. Channel 1 delay is 160 samples.

demos.title: Room RT Reference
demos.description: Two synthetic room responses with known reverberation times for RT60 regression and decay-curve sanity checks.
demos.sourceSummary: Perfect pink excitation mirrored to two outputs.
demos.systemSummary: Channel-specific room impulse responses with RT60 = 0.35 s and 0.62 s.
demos.notes: The direct path arrives earlier on channel 0 and the longer decay appears on channel 1.

demos.title: 2x2 Multi-Source Matrix
demos.description: Two interleaved excitation sources driving a 2x2 transfer matrix for source separation and cross-path validation.
demos.sourceSummary: Two direct-output perfect-white sources with interleaved frequency bins.
demos.systemSummary: A deterministic 2x2 path matrix with unique delays and gains for each source-to-mic route.
demos.notes: Designed to confirm that the extracted transfer matrix expands to four distinct paths.

demos.title: Nonlinear Harmonic Separation
demos.description: A two-channel matched-harmonic reference with known impulse locations for Zadoff-Chu order filtering.
demos.sourceSummary: Single Zadoff-Chu excitation mirrored to two outputs.
demos.systemSummary: Two channels with distinct first-, second-, and third-order matched harmonic kernels.
demos.notes: Designed to validate harmonic extraction and the grouped harmonic summary plot for two channels using the app's matched-filter Zadoff-Chu model.

demos.title: Polynomial Regression Joint Reference
demos.description: A deterministic Duffing-style equation-error fixture with known y, ydot, yddot, and y^3 coefficients.
demos.sourceSummary: Normalized synthetic forcing u(t) generated from a known polynomial ODE.
demos.systemSummary: Recorded y(t) is a multi-tone periodic state with analytic derivatives.
demos.notes: Use the coefficient bars to confirm y, ydot, yddot, and y^3 dominate the fit.

demos.title: Polynomial Regression Matched Reference
demos.description: A Zadoff-Chu polynomial harmonic fixture for validating the per-order matched regression path.
demos.systemSummary: First-, second-, and third-order polynomial harmonic response generated with known circular kernels.
demos.notes: Use this demo to validate that POLYREGRESSION_MATCHED runs in the worker and updates VIS_POLYFIT for H1/H2/H3.
